title: Experimental evidence of the topological obstruction in twisted bilayer graphene
type1_summary: The two PDF files are Nature source-data supplements and are best classified as cleaned, figure-supporting Type 1 files.
type2_summary: The Zenodo STM archive and all extracted .sxm files are raw instrument measurements, clearly Type 2.
classification_notes: The dataset clearly contains both cleaned source-data supplements and raw STM instrument files. The paper explicitly supports this mixed structure: raw measurements were deposited in Zenodo, while processed FFT/QPI and dispersion products appear in the manuscript and source-data PDFs.

file_classifications.reasoning: This file is best interpreted as cleaned, figure-supporting source data. The paper emphasizes processed outputs and the file source is Nature source data, which strongly favors Type 1 over raw data.
file_classifications.ambiguity: The PDF contents were not directly inspected, so exact internal structure is unknown; classification relies on source and paper context.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) with scanning tunneling microscopy/spectroscopy near a point defect to probe quasiparticle interference (QPI) and the local density of states. The key finding is that the observed 2q-arcs in Fourier-transformed LDOS are evidence that the two Dirac cones within a moiré mini-valley have the same chirality, confirming the topological obstruction of a two-band Wannier description; the data also reconstruct the band dispersion and Lifshitz transition near the van Hove singularity.

figures.description: Schematic of the twisted graphene Brillouin zones, moiré mini-Brillouin zone, and the same-chirality Dirac cones within each mini-valley.
STM topograph of 4.3° twisted bilayer graphene showing the moiré pattern and a point defect near the center.
Real-space LDOS map around the defect showing periodic quasiparticle interference modulations.
Schematic of intercone back-scattering processes and the expected FFT signatures for same- versus opposite-chirality Dirac cones.
Map of defect positions within the moiré unit cell used for comparing different scatterers.
FFT magnitude of the LDOS map showing circles/arcs centered at moiré reciprocal-space points.
Experimental dI/dV spectra at several defect positions compared with theoretical LDOS spectra.
figures.likely_source: mixed: raw measurement plus derived/theory overlay

figures.description: Tight-binding simulated STM/QPI images and corresponding FFTs for different defect types/positions and with/without heterostrain.
Tight-binding simulation for a non-reconstructed carbon vacancy defect, showing weaker QPI signal.
Energy-dependent experimental QPI maps in real space and reciprocal space across the van Hove singularity.
Calculated constant-energy contours of pristine TBG across the Lifshitz transition, with indicated scattering vectors.
Unfolded band-contour/scattering schematic in the original graphene valleys, highlighting intra-mini-valley scattering.
Reconstructed dispersion from experimental QPI arc radii compared with tight-binding calculations, including heterostrain/no-strain bands.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw STM measurements acquired in a custom-built UHV STM at 8.4 K and 10^-10 mbar: topography images, spatially resolved dI/dV (LDOS) maps, and spectroscopy at selected points around point defects in twisted bilayer graphene on SiC. The STM tip was Pt/Ir, and dI/dV maps were acquired with lock-in detection using a 10 mV AC modulation.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: The paper presents processed LDOS maps, FFTs of the LDOS/QPI images, extracted 2q-arc radii versus energy, reconstructed band dispersion, and comparisons to tight-binding simulations. These are derived from the raw STM/STS data by spatial Fourier transform, arc fitting/width extraction, and energy-dependent analysis.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The STM data measured in this study have been deposited in the Zenodo database and can be accessed at https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17360113.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: The paper explicitly says the STM data measured in the study are deposited in Zenodo, but it does not specify file formats, folder structure, or whether the deposit contains only raw scans or also intermediate products.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include LDOS maps, FFT-QPI images, extracted dispersion points from 2q-arc radii, and spectra compared to theory. The paper also discusses tight-binding calculations and simulated QPI images, but it does not state that these are deposited as separate data files.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly shown in the manuscript (real-space images, FFTs, spectra, and dispersion points), but the text does not explicitly state that per-figure source data files are provided. The Zenodo deposit likely contains the STM data, but the exact organization is not described.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned in the provided text. Tight-binding and T-matrix calculations are described in the paper, but script availability is unknown.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper states that STM data are deposited in Zenodo, which suggests the measurements are available for reanalysis. However, the text does not specify whether the deposit includes raw scans, processed figure source data, or scripts, so direct replot readiness cannot be fully verified from the paper alone.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is centered on STM/STS measurements of a TBG sample with a point defect, plus derived QPI/FFT and dispersion extraction. The manuscript distinguishes experimental data from tight-binding simulations and theoretical LDOS/QPI calculations. It also mentions supplementary sections for defect modeling, heterostrain, and additional samples, implying additional figure-related datasets may exist, but their exact contents are not described in the text.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper explicitly states that STM data measured in the study were deposited in Zenodo, and the main results are based on raw STM topographs, LDOS maps, and dI/dV spectroscopy.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The manuscript contains many processed outputs: FFT-QPI maps, extracted dispersion points, and comparison plots to tight-binding calculations, which are typical source-data-style figure products.
classification_prior.both_evidence: The text implies both raw experimental STM data and processed figure data are part of the study, but it does not explicitly enumerate the contents of the Zenodo deposit.
data_availability_section_relevant: The STM data measured in this study have been deposited in the Zenodo database
Modulus of the Fast Fourier Transform
The energy dependence of the QPIs
classification_prior.review_notes: Be careful not to conflate experimental STM data with theoretical tight-binding simulations. The paper clearly contains both, but only the experimental STM data are explicitly said to be deposited. The exact Zenodo contents, file formats, and whether scripts are included remain unknown from the provided text.
